item.title: Targeting this $2.8 trillion tax shelter could solve a big U.S. problem
item.description: Only good can come from taxing these “nonprofits.”

item.title: The median age of home buyers is an affront to the American Dream
item.description: The model pioneered by Health Savings Accounts can help families achieve home ownership.

item.title: These diseases could be consigned to the dustbin of history — thanks to the U.S.
item.description: With new funding, America can continue to lead the historic effort to eliminate ancient scourges.

item.title: Humanity’s superpower shines through this iconic image
item.description: Jack Kirby’s legacy shows how AI can’t mimic the creative power of human imagination.

item.title: The best education for future success might surprise you
item.description: Career-focused programs mistakenly assume that what seems useful today will be useful tomorrow.

item.title: This U.S. tour should be a civic priority
item.description: Planes, trains and automobiles bring celebrations to cities across the country.

item.title: China’s bullying has Japan up in arms
item.description: The prime minister is moving quickly to establish Tokyo as a military power in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: How the left punishes small business
item.description: Raising the top tax rate would affect a lot of unsuspecting middle-class workers.

item.title: The Potomac sewage spill is devastating — it’s also disturbingly common
item.description: Readers on the Potomac sewage spill, D.C.’s bravest and nuclear fuel reprocessing

item.title: At last, the credibility of U.S. deterrence is being restored
item.description: The perhaps 30,000 protesters who perished in Iran’s streets in early January did not die in vain.

item.title: The economy is changing. Don’t forget who fears it most.
item.description: Artificial intelligence doomer narratives reflect personal concerns, not economic reality.

item.title: Germany’s chancellor sets the standard for negotiating with China
item.description: As Friedrich Merz flies to D.C., it’s in America’s interest to shore up trading ties with the world’s third-biggest economy.

item.title: One and done? Trump’s Iran operation is unfortunately the opposite.
item.description: Khamenei may be dead — but war with Iran isn’t over.

item.title: Thanks to President Trump, the hour of Iran’s freedom is at hand
item.description: Our path forward is a new constitution followed by free elections under international oversight.

item.title: Trump’s Iran gamble
item.description: It’s too early to call the riskiest foreign policy decision of this term a failure or success.

item.title: The FDA shifts the goalposts on rare disease drugs
item.description: Agency leaders say the right things, but their actions tell a different story.
